propositions: Parallel agent sessions race to land on fast-forward-only main, forcing manual rebase-to-tip retries that burn CI re-runs and agent attention.
A serial auto-lander rebases the front Autosubmit change/chain to tip, awaits a fresh Verified +1, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA without weakening the two-vote gate.
Agents interact via a single land command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) so they never correlate Gerrit votes/labels themselves.
On failure the whole stack is handed back to its owning agent — never partial-land, never evict a change from within a stack.
All landing logic lives in project infra (Gerrit + GitHub Actions) and must add no Gerrit-specific landing logic to platform-agnostic src/rebar core.
Cross-stack batching is rejected/deferred to bounded speculation, and the platform-agnostic landing provider-seam is deferred to a separate idea.
